subject: Human Resources
title: Employment Contracts and Handbooks
description: A quick win: understand Employment Contracts and Handbooks.

hook.text: A company fires an employee. The employee sues—citing the handbook's promise of 'progressive discipline.' The company loses.

buildup.text: Employment contracts formalize the relationship. Handbooks set policies. Both can create legally binding obligations.

discovery.text: Courts have ruled that handbook language—if specific enough—can override at-will employment and create implied contracts.

twist.text: Many companies add disclaimers: 'This handbook is not a contract.' But inconsistent enforcement can still create liability.

climax.text: Well-drafted handbooks protect companies. Poorly drafted ones become ammunition for employees' lawyers.

punchline.text: Every word in a handbook is a promise—or a liability.

quiz.question: Why can employee handbooks create legal risk?
options: Specific language can create implied contracts overriding at-will employment
